title: Python, Tcl, Tkinter, Threads, and the Event Loop · Runtime Lens: Single Root Mainloop
summary: The interface creates multiple roots, blocks callbacks with work, calls widgets from an arbitrary thread, and exits with scheduled callbacks still alive. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the single root mainloop increment in single-root event-loop lifecycle contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create one application root, initialize state before mainloop, and shut down widgets and callbacks deliberately.
Trace Python calls through tkinter into one Tcl interpreter and the Tk widget toolkit with recorded patch levels.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-single-root-mainloop — Create one application root, initialize state before mainloop, and shut down widgets and callbacks deliberately.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-runtime-tk-event-loop-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-runtime-tk-event-loop-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_runtime_tk_event_loop_2(roots: int = 1, pending_callbacks: int = 0, longest_callback_ms: int = 7, ui_thread_owned: bool = True, clean_shutdown: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if roots != 1:
        return False, roots, "root-lifecycle"
    if pending_callbacks < 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "callback-count"
    if longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16:
        return False, longest_callback_ms, "blocking-callback"
    if not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "runtime-ownership"
    assert roots == 1 and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, longest_callback_ms, "cooperative-runtime"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-single-root-mainloop-v307q1
# Competency: maze-single-root-mainloop.
# Case 8769: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def evidence_maze_single_root_mainloop_v307q1(roots: int = 1, pending_callbacks: int = 0, longest_callback_ms: int = 7, ui_thread_owned: bool = True, clean_shutdown: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_v307q1 = "v307q1-1q703v-8769"
    assert evidence_variant_v307q1
    evidence_axes_v307q1 = (
        "gridv307q1",
        "topology8769",
        "search1q703v",
        "eventv307q18769",
        "access8769v307q1",
        "recovery1q703v8769",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_v307q1)) == 6
    if roots != 1:
        return False, roots, "root-lifecycle"
    if pending_callbacks < 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "callback-count"
    if longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16:
        return False, longest_callback_ms, "blocking-callback"
    if not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "runtime-ownership"
    assert roots == 1 and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, longest_callback_ms, "cooperative-runtime"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Single Root Mainloop model · Theory Single Root Mainlo…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Single Root Mainloop: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 8467 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to model a single-root cooperative event loop with bounded callbacks and deliberate shutdown; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Create one application root, initialize state before mainloop, and shut down widgets and callbacks deliberately.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Single Root Mainloop model · Theory Single Root Mainlo….
steps.why: Retrieving Single Root Mainloop through the concrete single-root event-loop lifecycle contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Single Root Mainloop model · Theory Single Root Mainlo…, keep Single Root Mainloop connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create one application root, initialize state before mainloop, and shut down widgets and callbacks deliberately.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Single Root Mainloop model · Theory Single Root Mainlo…, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Single Root Mainloop precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Single Root Mainloop model · Theory Single Root Mainlo…, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 8467 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to model a single-root cooperative event loop with bounded callbacks and deliberate shutdown; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Create one application root, initialize state before mainloop, and shut down widgets and callbacks deliberately.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Single Root Mainloop model · Theory Single Root Mainlo…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Every dialog should create its own independent Tk root.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-single-root-mainloop-v307q1.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-single-root-mainloop-v307q1(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_single_root_mainloop_v307q1\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?roots != 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pending_callbacks < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
